title: Craniofacial Conditions
authors: G. Kyle Fulton, Matthew S. Blessing & Kelly N. Evans

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Craniofacial Conditions?
Craniofacial malformations เป็น life-threatening condition ที่ neonatologist เจอใน delivery room. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) Robin sequence: triad micrognathia + glossoptosis + airway obstruction — tongue = ball valve → respiratory emergency; (2) Bilateral choanal atresia ใน CHARGE: neonate obligate nose breather → cyanosis ที่ดีขึ้นเมื่อร้องไห้ = pathognomonic → oral airway ทันที; (3) 22q11.2DS: conotruncal CHD + neonatal hypocalcemia + seizures = most common microdeletion (1:4,000); (4) Syndromic craniosynostosis: tracheal cartilaginous sleeve ใน Crouzon/Pfeiffer = rigid trachea → ชีวิตเสี่ยง; (5) BWS: macroglossia + hyperinsulinism hypoglycemia (30–60%) + Wilms tumor risk 7.5% → abdominal US q3 months; (6) CNPAS: PA < 11 mm + solitary median central incisor → HPE spectrum → pituitary dysfunction 40%.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
วินิจฉัย Robin sequence จาก triad + U-shaped cleft palate; แยก isolated RS vs syndromic RS
จัดการ airway obstruction ใน RS: prone → NP airway → TPP/OAP → TLA → MDO → tracheostomy
รู้ Stickler syndrome: COL2A1/COL11A1, RS + myopia + retinal detachment risk, ตรวจตา 6–12 เดือน
จำแนก cleft lip ± palate vs cleft palate only; recurrence risk; feeding ด้วย cleft feeder
อธิบาย 22q11.2DS: 1:4,000, CHD7 ≠ CHD7 (คนละอัน), cardiac + hypocalcemia + immune + palatal dysfunction
รู้ craniosynostosis: single suture (sagittal 60%, metopic 20–30%, coronal 10–20%, lambdoid 1–3%) vs syndromic
รู้ 5 craniosynostosis syndromes: Apert (FGFR2), Crouzon (FGFR2), Pfeiffer (FGFR1/2), Muenke (FGFR3), Saethre-Chotzen (TWIST1)
วินิจฉัย CHARGE: CHD7, major criteria 4 ข้อ (coloboma, choanal atresia, CN dysfunction, CHARGE ear), bilateral choanal atresia = neonatal emergency
รู้ BWS: 11p15.5, macroglossia + hyperinsulinism hypoglycemia + omphalocele + lateralized overgrowth; tumor surveillance
วินิจฉัย CNPAS: PA < 11 mm, solitary median central incisor, pituitary dysfunction 40%, brain MRI ทุกราย
อธิบาย CFM (OMENS) / Treacher Collins / Moebius syndrome — ICU airway + feeding concerns
รู้ prenatal screening: 2D US ตรวจ CL±P ได้ 9–100%, CPO ได้ 0–22%; EXIT procedure ใน severe airway compromise
1. Framework — Craniofacial Malformations กับ Neonatologist
1.1 Scope of Impact
Craniofacial malformations กระทบ: swallowing, breathing, hearing, vision, speech, development; บางรายทำให้เกิด life-threatening airway compromise ตั้งแต่แรกคลอด
Neonatologist = first point of contact; ต้องรู้ว่าเมื่อไหร่ต้อง act เองและเมื่อไหร่ต้อง consult craniofacial team
1.2 Multidisciplinary Craniofacial Team

Core members: Audiology, Dentistry, Feeding specialist, Genetics, Neurosurgery, Nursing, Oral Surgery, Orthodontics, Otolaryngology, Pediatrics, Plastics/Craniofacial Surgery, Social Work, Speech-Language Pathology
Ancillary (guided by patient needs): Cardiology, GI, Neurodevelopmental Medicine, Ophthalmology, Psychology, Pulmonology, Sleep Medicine
ACPA-accredited teams: acpa-cpf.org/acpa-family-services/find-a-team/; referral ควรเกิดขึ้น ภายใน 1 สัปดาห์หลัง discharge
2. Robin Sequence — Micrognathia/Glossoptosis/Airway Obstruction
2.1 Diagnosis, Etiology, Prevalence
Triad: micrognathia + glossoptosis + upper airway obstruction = Robin sequence (RS); cleft palate พบร่วมด้วย ~90% (U-shaped cleft = classic; V-shaped = non-RS)
Prevalence: 1:24,500 (range 1:3,900–1:122,400 ใน 42 international studies; Paes et al. 2015)
Etiology: heterogeneous; large cohort 191 children (Basart et al. 2015): 38% isolated RS, 9% chromosomal, 29% Mendelian disorder (Stickler syndrome most common), 24% no detectable cause
Classification: "isolated RS" vs "RS plus" (RS + other anomalies) vs "syndromic RS"
2.2 Phenotype
Micrognathia: small, symmetrically receded mandible; assess maxillomandibular discrepancy (distance between maxillary and mandibular alveolar ridges) objectively
Glossoptosis: displacement of tongue base into oropharynx/hypopharynx; dynamic; severity ≠ correlates with degree of micrognathia
Airway obstruction: stertor, increased inspiratory effort, obstructive apnea, cyanosis; worse supine + during feeding + in sleep
2.3 ICU Concerns
Tongue = ball valve mechanism: tongue displacement toward posterior pharyngeal wall → inspiratory obstruction → ↑ WOB, cyanosis, apnea
Rising CO₂ = early signal of worsening airway obstruction — often precedes hypoxemia; trending CO₂ + SpO₂ = minimum monitoring (Ross & Weissberg, Avery 2024)
Chronic obstruction → failure to thrive + CO₂ retention + pulmonary hypertension + cor pulmonale
Feeding issues: airway obstruction + swallowing dysfunction + pharyngeal hypotonia + cleft palate suction mechanics; GER พบบ่อย
Difficult intubation: micrognathia → compromised airway exposure → plan airway ก่อน procedure ทุกครั้ง
Congenital heart defects: up to 25% ของ RS ที่เสียชีวิตใน early infancy
2.4 Management — Airway Escalation Ladder

RS Airway Escalation Protocol
Step 1: Prone/lateral decubitus position → ↑ airway patency (first-line). Step 2: Nasopharyngeal (NP) airway — ETT ดัดแปลงผ่าน nares เข้า hypopharynx เหนือ epiglottis; diagnostic + therapeutic; บางรายกลับบ้านได้พร้อม NP airway + parent training. Step 3: Intraoral plate (TPP/OAP) — Tubingen palatal plate / orthodontic airway plate; well-established ใน Europe; ช่วย tongue position, อนุญาต full oral feeding. Step 4: Tongue-lip adhesion (TLA) — temporizing measure. Step 5: Mandibular distraction osteogenesis (MDO): surgical osteotomy + distraction device → ↑ mandibular length → tongue forward; ใช้ได้ถ้า isolated base of tongue obstruction ไม่มี multilevel obstruction. Step 6: Tracheostomy: gold standard ใน severe/multilevel obstruction, failure ของ less invasive intervention, CNS/skeletal comorbidities.
Airway endoscopy + CT facial skeleton: ช่วย delineate level of obstruction + jaw anatomy ก่อน MDO
Nutrition: cleft feeder + side-lying position + fortified breast milk/formula; NG/OG feeding ถ้า airway ไม่ stable; feeding therapist consult; gastrostomy พบบ่อยใน syndromic RS
2.5 Stickler Syndrome — Most Common Syndrome with RS
~1/3 ของ RS มี Stickler syndrome; autosomal dominant connective tissue disorder; มักมี variable expressivity
Features: RS + cleft palate, high myopia + retinal detachment risk + blindness (preventable!), midface hypoplasia, flat nasal bridge, hearing loss (sensorineural/conductive), arthropathy, scoliosis
Genes: > 90% มี mutation ใน COL2A1 หรือ COL11A1; clinical molecular testing sensitive + available

Stickler Syndrome Surveillance Protocol
สำหรับ neonatal RS ทุกราย: (1) Ophthalmology evaluation 6–12 เดือน เพื่อตรวจหา ocular features ของ Stickler (myopia, cataracts); (2) Hearing assessment annually; (3) Developmental monitoring + early intervention referral; (4) Sleep study ถ้าสงสัย OSA. Retinal detachment prevention-focused: ophthalmology surveillance ตลอดชีวิต เมื่อวินิจฉัย SS แล้ว.
3. Orofacial Clefts — Cleft Lip and/or Palate
3.1 Epidemiology
Cleft lip + palate (CL±P): 1:1,563 births; cleft lip only: 1:2,807; cleft palate only (CPO): 1:1,687; US = ~17 babies/day
3.2 Anatomy & Etiology
Primary palate: lip + alveolus + anterior hard palate (to incisive foramen); fuses by week 7 of gestation; failure = CL±P
Secondary palate: posterior hard palate + soft palate; CL±P และ CPO distinct etiologies
Nonsyndromic (isolated): 70–75% ของ CL±P; ~55% ของ CPO; multifactorial (genetic + environment)
Risk factors: maternal tobacco + alcohol + anticonvulsants (valproate); nutritional status; some evidence for preconception folate supplementation protective
Bifid uvula: พบ 2–4% ของ births — อาจเป็น sign ของ submucous cleft palate (muscle defect with intact mucosa) — functional impact เหมือน overt CP
3.3 ICU Concerns
Cleft palate (CP): feeding difficulties + nasal regurgitation; eustachian tube dysfunction + otitis media risk; speech issues; GER พบบ่อย
Midline cleft: evaluate for HPE (holoprosencephaly); midline cleft + premaxillary agenesis + hypoteloric appearance = HPE until proven otherwise; CT/MRI brain ทุกราย

Opitz G/BBB syndrome: hypertelorism (100%) + CL±P (50%) + laryngotracheoesophageal (LTE) defects (70%) → stridor + aspiration risk; AD + XLR forms; gold standard diagnosis: microlaryngoscopy + bronchoscopy
3.4 Feeding & Surgical Management
Cleft feeder: specialized compressible bottle (Haberman, Dr Brown's special needs); side-lying position; feeding therapist consult ทุกราย
Surgery timeline: cleft lip repair < 6 months; palatoplasty 9–12 months (goal: normalize palate muscle function → normal speech development)
Follow-up: craniofacial specialist ภายใน 1 สัปดาห์จาก discharge; genetics consult ถ้า syndromic features
4. 22q11.2 Deletion Syndrome (DiGeorge/Velocardiofacial Syndrome)
4.1 Diagnosis & Prevalence
Most common microdeletion syndrome: 1:4,000 births; deletion typically 3 Mb, ~40 genes on one copy of chromosome 22; associated with > 180 clinical features

Neonatal indications for testing: conotruncal heart disease (tetralogy, interrupted aortic arch, truncus arteriosus), neonatal hypocalcemia + seizures, dysphagia/nasal regurgitation, CP, submucous CP, thymic hypo-/aplasia
4.2 Phenotype
Craniofacial: subtle in neonatal period; small ears + overfolded helices, long face, tubular nose (squared nasal tip), nasal alar hypoplasia, hooded eyelids
Cardiac: ~2/3 ของผู้ป่วย; conotruncal + aortic arch malformations most common → prolonged NICU stay
Palatal dysfunction: CP/submucous CP/velopharyngeal insufficiency → nasal regurgitation แม้ไม่มี overt CP
4.3 ICU Concerns

22q11.2DS: สามอาการที่ NICU ต้องสงสัย
Triad ที่ต้องทำ 22q11.2 testing ทันที: (1) Conotruncal CHD + (2) Neonatal seizures → hypocalcemia (hypoparathyroidism; most common in neonatal period and triggered by physiologic stress) + (3) Dysphagia/nasal regurgitation ± CP. Hypocalcemia + neonatal seizures → linked with worse intellectual outcomes (Shprintzen 2008). Airway obstruction (tracheomalacia, subglottic stenosis, laryngomalacia) พบโดยเฉพาะใน patients ที่มี CHD ร่วมด้วย.
Immune: T-cell deficiency (thymic hypoplasia) → detected on NBS by TRECs; severe immunodeficiency rare but serious
Urinary tract: ~1/3 มี structural anomalies; cervical spine anomalies → monitor สำหรับ cord compression symptoms
4.4 Management
ECG + echo + cardiology consult; calcium + PTH levels; CBC + flow cytometry T/B cells; renal US ทุก confirmed/suspected cases
Palatal examination ทุกราย; diagnostic hearing test; ophthalmology evaluation; thyroid function via NBS
Feeding therapist evaluation + swallow study ถ้ามี dysphagia; genetic counseling ทุกครอบครัว

Long-term: multidisciplinary team (cardiology, immunology, behavioral health, psychiatry, audiology, endocrinology); พัฒนาการ + psychiatric risk ↑ ใน older children/young adults: depression, anxiety, ADHD, schizophrenia
5. Craniosynostosis — Premature Suture Fusion
5.1 Overview
Prevalence: 1:2,500 live births (recent data suggesting increase to 1:1,400; Dias et al. Pediatrics 2020); restricted growth perpendicular to fused suture + compensatory growth in patent sutures
Etiologies: monogenic + chromosomal + polygenic + environmental/teratogenic; genetic diagnosis identified in 25%; nonsyndromic single suture = 65% of patients
Diagnosis: clinical exam by experienced craniofacial provider sufficient; CT confirms + surgical planning; plain skull radiographs in neonates = unreliable
5.2 Single Suture Synostosis

rows: Frontal bossing + biparietal narrowing + occipital elongation; male > female; distinguish from breech dolichocephaly (normalizes; sagittal progresses)
Midline forehead ridge + frontotemporal narrowing + hypotelorism; valproate exposure risk; ~25% have syndrome/chromosomal anomaly
Flat supraorbital rim + higher orbit ipsilateral + frontal bulge contralateral; nose twists away from fusion; genetics consult + test FGFR2/3, TWIST1, TCF12, EFNB1
Ipsilateral occiput flat + inferior-posterior ear displacement + mastoid bulge + skull base tilt (absent in positional plagiocephaly!)

blocks.text: 5.3 Syndromic Craniosynostosis — Major Syndromes

rows: Acrobrachycephaly; proptosis; trapezoid mouth; invariable symmetric syndactyly hands + feet; TES fistula (rare); maxillary hypoplasia → OSA; moderate-severe cognitive impairment
Proptosis (shallow orbits); Chiari type 1 + hydrocephalus; progressive ICP; cognitive usually normal; solid cartilaginous trachea → rigid airway; corneal lubrication for proptosis
Broad deviated thumbs + large first toes; Types I–III (cloverleaf skull = type II/III); solid cartilaginous trachea; spine/CNS anomalies; complex airway
Macrocephaly; NO significant midface hypoplasia (unlike Apert/Crouzon); high prevalence sensorineural hearing loss
Low frontal hairline; ptosis; 2–3 syndactyly fingers; duplicated halluces; cervical spine anomalies; usually normal intelligence (point mutations); deletion = significant delay

blocks.text: 5.4 ICU Concerns ใน Craniosynostosis
Airway compromise: midface hypoplasia → ↓ nasal/oropharyngeal space → obstructive sleep apnea + asphyxia + death; most dangerous: Apert, Pfeiffer, Crouzon

⚠️ Tracheal Cartilaginous Sleeve — Life-Threatening
Crouzon และ Pfeiffer syndromes: vertically fused tracheal cartilage = solid cartilaginous trachea (stovepipe trachea) → rigid airway → upper airway stenosis + inability to clear secretions + ↑ risk of injury from decreased distensibility. อาจ extend จาก subglottis ถึง carina/bronchus. ต้องวินิจฉัย ก่อน intubation/tracheostomy: direct laryngoscopy/bronchoscopy + MRI/CT + ultrasound (emerging tool). Tracheostomy ใน tracheal cartilaginous sleeve = high risk ของ granulation tissue + abnormal healing — complex airway surgery อาจจำเป็น.
ICP: chronic elevated ICP → symptomatic intracranial hypertension ช่วง brain growth rapid (first 2 years); ICP ใน neonate ไม่ค่อย life-threatening เพราะ open fontanel + compensatory erosion
Hydrocephalus: Crouzon + Pfeiffer > others; Chiari type 1 malformation พบบ่อยใน syndromic; Chiari → central sleep apnea; treatment ของ obstructive apnea อาจ unmask central apnea
Proptosis: ocular lubricants prevent exposure keratopathy; severe proptosis → globe luxation → tarsorrhaphy
5.5 Management
Physical exam: sutural ridging + skull base symmetry + ear position + fontanelles + proptosis + limb anomalies + heart + cleft palate

CT 3D reconstruction: confirms diagnosis + suture extent + surgical planning; surgery generally first year of life; techniques: endoscopic strip craniectomy, calvarial distraction, traditional cranioplasty; Mathijssen 2021 comprehensive guideline
Genetics: single suture role evolving; multisuture → genetics consultation + molecular testing + counseling ทุกราย; WES/WGS ถ้า panel negative; ~20 known hereditary forms
6. Disorders of the First and Second Branchial Arches
6.1 Craniofacial Microsomia (CFM)
Most common facial asymmetry; prevalence 1:3,000–1:5,600; asymmetric deficiency of skeletal + soft tissue — ipsilateral mandible + ear most affected
Etiology: multifactorial, mostly sporadic; recurrence risk ~2% (higher if known family history of microtia/CFM)
OMENS classification: Orbital distortion + Mandibular hypoplasia + Ear anomaly + Nerve involvement + Soft tissue deficiency
Extracraniofacial anomalies (47% of cases): vertebral 28%, cardiac 21% (septal defects, ToF), CNS 11%, urogenital 11%, GI 9%; higher in bilateral CFM
Goldenhar syndrome: CFM subgroup + vertebral anomalies + epibulbar dermoids
ICU: unilateral/bilateral mandibular hypoplasia → upper airway obstruction; prone positioning helps mild cases; tracheostomy ถ้า severe
Screening: hearing test at birth + follow-up; renal US; echo ถ้า murmur; ophthalmology (colobomas, epibulbar lipodermoids, keratopathy); cervical spine x-ray deferred to 2–3 years old
6.2 Moebius Syndrome
Rare (~2,000 people worldwide); CN VI + VII palsies universal: inability to abduct eyes (CN VI) + facial paralysis (CN VII)
Masklike facies — ยากจะ recognize ใน newborn period; feeding difficulties from swallowing dysfunction + aspiration + palatal weakness; high aspiration risk
Exposure conjunctivitis/keratopathy ใน lagophthalmos → ocular lubricants; limb defects > 50% (talipes deformity most common); association with Poland anomaly (absent pectoralis)
6.3 Treacher Collins Syndrome (TCS)
Prevalence: 1:50,000 live births; first + second branchial arch tissues affected

Major features: hypoplasia of mandible + zygoma, microtia, external auditory canal atresia, bilateral conductive hearing loss, downslanting palpebral fissures, lower eyelid colobomas; cleft palate; hearing loss ≤50%
Genes: TCOF1 (71–93%), POLR1B, POLR1C, POLR1D; genetic testing confirms diagnosis; extra­craniofacial features rare

TCS vs CFM: Airway Risk
TCS: bilateral + symmetric mandibular hypoplasia → higher risk of upper airway obstruction vs CFM; choanal stenosis/atresia พบร่วมด้วยใน TCS → multilevel airway obstruction → MDO อาจไม่เพียงพอ → tracheostomy บ่อยกว่า; risk of death ใน neonatal period. Airway endoscopy ทุกรายที่มี airway obstruction signs ใน TCS + CFM.
7. CHARGE Syndrome
7.1 Diagnosis & Prevalence

Prevalence: 1:10,000 births; mutations in CHD7 gene account for most cases (chromodomain helicase DNA-binding protein 7); clinical diagnosis — some meet criteria without CHD7 mutation; further testing warranted if CHD7 negative
CHARGE = Coloboma + Heart defect + Atresia choanae + Retardation of growth + Genital hypoplasia + Ear anomalies/deafness; coined by Pagon
7.2 Clinical Diagnosis Criteria

rows: Cranial nerve dysfunction (I, VII, VIII, IX, X) (40–90%)

blocks.text: Strongly suspected: all 4 major criteria OR 3 major + 3 minor criteria.
7.3 ICU Emergency — Bilateral Choanal Atresia

⚠️ Bilateral Choanal Atresia = Neonatal Emergency
Neonates = obligate nose breathers; bilateral posterior choanal atresia → cyanosis + respiratory distress ภายใน 1 ชั่วโมงแรก. Pathognomonic clue: ร้องไห้แล้วหายเขียว (crying relieves cyanosis) — เพราะ air เข้าทาง mouth แทน nose. Feeding ทำให้อาการแย่ลง. Left untreated → asphyxiation + death. การจัดการ: (1) Oral airway ทันที; (2) ถ้าไม่พอ → ETT intubation; (3) CT nasal passages (ยืนยัน) + CT temporal bones (Mondini cochlea malformation + absent/hypoplastic semicircular canals) — characteristic inner ear findings ของ CHARGE; (4) Trans-nasal stents หลัง choanal repair.
Unilateral choanal atresia/stenosis: อาจไม่แสดงอาการจนหลัง newborn period — chronic rhinorrhea + nasal obstruction เวลา URI
Cardiac: echo ทันที (cyanotic CHD: ToF, outflow tract anomalies, interrupted aortic arch); threshold ต่ำมาก
Swallowing dysfunction + cranial nerve palsies (V, IX, X) + TEF → aspiration risk สูง; feeding specialist consult; NG/NJ/GJ feeds ถ้า aspiration concern
Mortality: 13% ของผู้ป่วย CHARGE (study 77 individuals; Issekutz et al. 2005); bilateral choanal atresia + cyanotic CHD + CNS malformations = ↑ mortality
7.4 Management & Surveillance
Immune: CBC + lymphocyte differential + calcium (overlap 22q11.2DS); immunologist ถ้ามี recurrent infections
Audiologic + ophthalmologic evaluation in neonatal period; early intervention referral

Surveillance (Trider et al. Pediatr Rev 2017): gonadotropins screening 3 months; lymphopenia + hypocalcemia screen; deaf-blind resource referral; cochlear implant assessment; GI motility treatment; neurodevelopmental support
8. Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome (BWS) — Macroglossia
8.1 Genetics & Diagnosis

Prevalence: 1:10,340 live births; genetics complex: chromosomal rearrangement, mutations, epigenetic effects (DNA methylation) affecting imprinted genes on chromosome 11p15.5; ~80% มี 11p15.5 abnormality on clinical testing
IVF: higher risk ของ BWS; clinical suspicion alone = initiate management + tumor surveillance
Clinical scoring (score ≥ 4 = BWS diagnosis):

Cardinal features (2 pts each): macroglossia, omphalocele, lateralized overgrowth, multifocal/bilateral Wilms tumor or nephroblastomatosis, hyperinsulinism > 1 week needing escalated treatment, adrenal cortex cytomegaly/placental mesenchymal dysplasia/pancreatic adenomatosis

Suggestive features (1 pt each): birth weight > 2 SD above mean, facial nevus simplex, polyhydramnios/placentomegaly, ear creases/pits, neonatal hypoglycemia < 1 week, neuroblastoma/rhabdomyosarcoma/unilateral Wilms/hepatoblastoma/adrenocortical ca, nephro-/hepatomegaly, umbilical hernia/diastasis recti
8.2 ICU Concerns

BWS: สามอาการฉุกเฉินใน NICU
(1) Hypoglycemia (hyperinsulinism): พบ 30–60% ของ neonates ใน first days of life → standard hypoglycemia protocol → ถ้า refractory → endocrinologist + subtotal pancreatectomy อาจจำเป็น. (2) Macroglossia → airway obstruction: อาจเริ่มใน newborn period (severe macroglossia); tongue → occludes upper airway, feeding issues, aspiration; intubation caution (macroglossia ↓ airway exposure); tracheostomy ถ้า prolonged intubation needed. (3) Omphalocele: surgery first few days of life.
Polycythemia: potential marker of congenital Wilms tumor; treat if severe
Cardiac: 13–20% มี congenital heart disease (cardiomegaly, cardiomyopathy, PDA, PFO, ASD, VSD, long QT syndrome); ECG + echo ถ้า concern
Prematurity พบบ่อยใน BWS; mortality reported up to 21% (complications of prematurity + macroglossia)
8.3 Tumor Surveillance

BWS Tumor Risk — ต้องทำ Surveillance ทันทีหลังวินิจฉัย
Embryonal tumor risk = 7.5% (Wilms tumor, hepatoblastoma, neuroblastoma, rhabdomyosarcoma); 95% present ใน first 8 years. Abdominal ultrasound ทุก 3 เดือน จนถึง 8 ปี (หรือตาม genotype-specific guidelines — geneticist แนะนำตาม 11p15.5 abnormality ที่พบ). Genotype สำคัญมาก: differing cancer risk profiles → genetics consult ทันที.
9. Other Notable Craniofacial Conditions
9.1 Frontonasal Dysplasia (FND) / Hypertelorism / Encephalocele
FND = abnormal morphogenesis ของ frontonasal process → ocular hypertelorism (interpupillary distance > 4.5 cm ใน term) + broadening nasal root + midline facial cleft ± encephalocele/meningocele
CNS manifestations: frontonasal encephalocele/meningocele (most common), agenesis corpus callosum, abnormal neuronal migration; CNS anomalies → ↑ cognitive impairment

⚠️ FND: ห้ามใส่ NG tube โดยไม่ตรวจ imaging ก่อน
FND มี high incidence ของ frontonasal encephalocele; การ instrument nose/mouth (NG tube, suction catheter) ก่อน delineate CNS anatomy = brain injury risk. Rule: CT/MRI ก่อน instrumentation ทุกครั้ง ใน infant ที่มี hypertelorism/FND features. ถ้าต้อง emergent intubation → examine intraoral structures carefully ก่อนป้องกัน injury ต่อ herniated CNS.
Craniofrontonasal syndrome: X-linked (EFNB1 gene); females more severely affected; coronal craniosynostosis + hypertelorism + bifid nasal tip + grooved nails; usually normal intelligence
9.2 Congenital Nasal Pyriform Aperture Stenosis (CNPAS)
Rare: ~1:25,000 births; bony overgrowth of maxilla at pyriform aperture (PA) — narrowest part of nasal airway; first described 1989; case reports doubling in recent years

Diagnosis: CT maxillofacial — PA < 11 mm ใน term neonate = CNPAS (normal average 16.9 mm); anterior rhinoscopy reveals narrow nasal valve; difficulty/inability to pass 5–6 Fr catheter through PA → investigate
Unique phenotype clues: absent maxillary labial frenulum + solitary median maxillary central incisor (60%) — relatively unique to CNPAS

CNPAS + Solitary Median Central Incisor = HPE Spectrum → Check Pituitary
CNPAS = microform ของ holoprosencephaly spectrum; CNS anomalies: pituitary anomalies, corpus callosum abnormalities, Chiari I, optic nerve hypoplasia, olfactory bulb agenesis. Pituitary/hypothalamic dysfunction: 40% of patients (diabetes insipidus, adrenal insufficiency, hypothyroidism, GH deficiency, hypogonadism). Hypoglycemia + conjugated hyperbilirubinemia = highly predictive ของ pituitary dysfunction. Protocol: brain MRI ทุกราย; endocrinology consult; endocrine follow-up ≥ 1 year. Low/plateaued linear growth ที่ 1 year = predictor ของ long-term pituitary dysfunction.

Management: conservative (saline irrigation + intranasal steroids + topical decongestants) ≤ 2 weeks; failure → balloon dilation (maternal estrogen-related bony plasticity) or sublabial surgical widening (open each side for ≥ 3.5 mm ETT passage); nasal stents 2–4 weeks postop
10. Prenatal Screening for Fetal Face Anomalies
ACOG: routine US at 18–22 weeks with visualization of upper lip; adequate evaluation ของ facial structures ตั้งแต่ 16–17 weeks
2D US: CL±P detection 9–100%; CPO detection 0–22% (tongue obscures palate); 3D/4D US: CL±P 86–90%, CPO 0–89%

Fetal MRI (high-risk): ↑ accuracy ใน confirming anomalies + identifying extracraniofacial anomalies; used to predict need for immediate neonatal airway intervention (glossoptosis, micrognathia, craniofacial masses)

EXIT Procedure — When to Plan
เมื่อ fetal airway compromise anticipated by prenatal imaging: polyhydramnios + severe micrognathia, neck mass with tracheal compression, mass-induced neck extension, neck vessel compression, solid neck mass → coordinate delivery ที่ tertiary care center with neonatology + obstetrics + peds ORL + peds anesthesia สำหรับ ex utero intrapartum treatment (EXIT) procedure: maternal-fetal circulation maintained เป็น bridge ขณะ securing airway / resecting obstructing lesion / ECMO setup. Prenatal counseling กับ craniofacial team → ↓ negative parental perceptions + ↓ postpartum depression rates.
11. NICU Practical — Craniofacial Conditions ที่ NICU

Robin sequence airway assessment: ทันที: prone position trial → NP airway (NG tube modified) → capillary blood gas + CO₂ trending; rising CO₂ = worsening obstruction signal; ถ้าไม่ดีขึ้น → airway endoscopy → MDO consult หรือ tracheostomy

Cleft palate feeding: Haberman feeder / Dr Brown's special needs bottle + side-lying; feeding therapist day 1; fortify feeds; head circumference + weight q72 hours; NG ถ้า intake ไม่พอ; gastrostomy ถ้า syndromic RS

CHARGE bilateral choanal atresia: oral airway ก่อน → CT nasal + temporal bones → ENT consult → trans-nasal stents; echo ทันที; CBC + Ca + flow cytometry (22q-like immune overlap); audiologic + ophthalmic evaluation
22q11.2DS workup: CXR + ECG + echo + cardiology; Ca + PTH; CBC + T/B cell flow cytometry; renal US; palatal exam; hearing screen; ophthalmology; thyroid NBS; genetic counseling ทุกครอบครัว

Craniosynostosis syndrome airway: ถ้า stridor/snoring/apnea → sleep study; otolaryngology + airway endoscopy; identify tracheal cartilaginous sleeve ก่อน tracheostomy ใน Crouzon/Pfeiffer; ocular lubricants ทุกรายที่มี proptosis

BWS hypoglycemia: glucose monitoring q1–2 hours ใน 24 ชั่วโมงแรก; target glucose > 2.8 mmol/L; GIR escalate; ถ้าต้องการ GIR > 8–10 + refractory → endocrinologist; subcutaneous octreotide/diazoxide; pancreas imaging; abdominal US ทันที (tumor baseline)

CNPAS: NG หรือ OG → oral airway → CPAP/O₂; CT maxillofacial ยืนยัน; brain MRI; endocrine labs (glucose, cortisol, thyroid, IGF-1, insulin, electrolytes); intranasal steroids + saline 2 สัปดาห์ก่อนพิจารณา surgery

Difficult airway planning: ทุก craniofacial condition ที่มี micrognathia/midface hypoplasia/macroglossia → video laryngoscope + difficult airway cart ready; anesthesia/ENT informed before elective procedures
12. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — Cyanosis ที่ดีขึ้นเมื่อร้องไห้ = Bilateral Choanal Atresia จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
Neonate ร้องแล้วสีดีขึ้น = air เข้าทาง mouth แทน nose = bilateral posterior choanal atresia. สงสัย CHARGE syndrome ก่อนอื่น (1:10,000). Oral airway ทันที → stabilize → CT nasal passages (ยืนยัน) + CT temporal bones (Mondini cochlea, absent semicircular canals = CHARGE). อย่ารอ — neonate พึ่ง nasal airway 100%.

PEARL 2 — Robin Sequence: CO₂ Trends ก่อน SpO₂ Drops
ใน RS, rising CO₂ เป็น warning signal ที่มาก่อน hypoxemia. Trending venous/capillary CO₂ q4–8 hr = minimum monitoring. SpO₂ alone ไม่เพียงพอ — infant compensates hypercapnia นาน ก่อนจะ desaturate. Step up care เมื่อ CO₂ ↑ progressively แม้ SpO₂ ยัง okay.

PEARL 3 — 22q11.2DS: Conotruncal CHD + Neonatal Hypocalcemia Seizures = Test Now
22q11.2DS คือ most common microdeletion syndrome (1:4,000). Clinical trigger: conotruncal CHD (ToF, IAA, truncus) + hypocalcemia + neonatal seizures → ส่ง 22q11.2 testing (chromosomal microarray หรือ FISH). Hypocalcemia + seizure → linked with worse intellectual outcome → treat calcium rapidly. ยังต้องตรวจ submucous CP ทุกราย (แค่ visual exam อาจพลาด — palpate palate).

PEARL 4 — Syndromic Craniosynostosis: Check for Tracheal Cartilaginous Sleeve Before Any Airway Procedure
Crouzon + Pfeiffer = solid cartilaginous trachea (stovepipe) → rigid airway. Tracheostomy ใน tracheal cartilaginous sleeve = high-risk procedure: granulation tissue, abnormal healing, custom airway อาจจำเป็น. Diagnose ก่อนด้วย direct laryngoscopy/bronchoscopy + CT/MRI + ultrasound. แจ้ง ENT ก่อนทุก airway procedure ใน Crouzon/Pfeiffer.

PEARL 5 — BWS: Macroglossia + Hypoglycemia = Abdominal US ทุก 3 เดือนจนอายุ 8 ปี
BWS มี tumor risk 7.5% (Wilms, hepatoblastoma, neuroblastoma, rhabdomyosarcoma); 95% ปรากฏใน 8 ปีแรก. Abdominal US ทุก 3 เดือน เป็น standard surveillance. ยังต้องจำ: polycythemia ใน BWS neonate = potential marker ของ congenital Wilms tumor. Genetics consult ทันที — genotype กำหนด cancer risk profile + surveillance protocol ที่เหมาะสม.
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Basart H et al. Am J Med Genet A 2015;167A:1983: Etiology of RS in 191 Dutch children — 38% isolated, 9% chromosomal, 29% Mendelian (Stickler most common), 24% no cause; establishes heterogeneity framework.

Paes EC et al. Am J Med Genet A 2015;167A:1972: Birth prevalence of RS Netherlands 2000–2010 + 42 international studies review — mean prevalence 1:24,500 (range 1:3,900–1:122,400); highlights diagnostic variability.

Breugem CC, Evans KN et al. JAMA Pediatr 2016;170:894: Best practices for diagnosis and evaluation of RS — international clinical consensus report; treatment protocols; airway escalation framework; indications for MDO vs tracheostomy.

Lewis CW et al. AAP/Pediatrics 2017;139:e20170628: Primary care management of children with cleft lip and/or palate — cleft feeder technique, surgical timing (lip repair < 6 mo, palatoplasty 9–12 mo), surveillance.

McDonald-McGinn DM et al. Nat Rev Dis Primers 2015;1:15071: 22q11.2 deletion syndrome — comprehensive review; cardiac 63%, hypocalcemia neonatal period, immunodeficiency, psychiatric risk in adolescence/adults.

Dias MS et al. Pediatrics 2020;146:e2020015511: Identifying the misshapen head — AAP guidelines for craniosynostosis; CT imaging criteria; referral timing; distinguishing positional plagiocephaly from synostosis.

Mathijssen IMJ et al. Plast Reconstr Surg 2021;148:987e: Updated comprehensive guideline for craniosynostosis — surgical timing, endoscopic vs open techniques, distraction osteogenesis, surveillance recommendations.

Issekutz KA et al. Am J Med Genet A 2005;133A:228: CHARGE syndrome study of 77 patients — mortality 13%, age at death < 1 week to 9 years; bilateral choanal atresia + cyanotic CHD + CNS malformations = highest risk of death.

Trider CL et al. Pediatr Rev 2017;38:56 (Hudson A, Blake K): CHARGE syndrome health supervision guidelines — gonadotropins, lymphopenia, hypocalcemia, cochlear implant assessment, deaf-blind resources, GI motility management.

Brioude F et al. Nat Rev Endocrinol 2018;14:229: International consensus statement on BWS — clinical/molecular diagnosis, scoring criteria (cardinal 2 pts, suggestive 1 pt), tumor surveillance protocols, genotype-specific cancer risk.

Cielo CM et al. Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2016;21:254: Craniofacial disorders associated with airway obstruction in the neonate — review of RS, craniosynostosis syndromes, CFM, TCS, CHARGE; airway management algorithm.

Galluzzi F et al. Int J Pediatr Otorhinolaryngol 2021;144:110670: Congenital bony nasal cavity stenosis (CNPAS) — review of diagnosis, pituitary dysfunction (40%), management algorithm, balloon dilation vs sublabial surgery.

Birgfeld C, Heike C. Clin Plast Surg 2019;46:207: Craniofacial microsomia — OMENS classification, extracraniofacial anomalies (vertebral 28%, cardiac 21%, CNS 11%), management timeline, hearing amplification guidelines.

Aljerian A, Gilardino MS. Clin Plast Surg 2019;46:197: Treacher Collins syndrome — TCOF1 (71–93%), bilateral mandibular hypoplasia, high tracheostomy rate, neonatal death risk, multilevel airway evaluation approach.

ACOG Practice Bulletin / AIUM 2020 (fetal anatomy US): Routine fetal anatomy US 18–22 weeks includes upper lip visualization; 2D CL±P detection 9–100%, CPO 0–22%; 3D/4D US 86–90% CL±P; fetal MRI in high-risk pregnancies.
14. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Robin sequence คืออะไร? ผล lab/imaging อะไรที่ต้อง monitor อย่างน้อยตลอดการรักษา?

ตอบ: RS = triad: micrognathia + glossoptosis + upper airway obstruction; cleft palate พบ ~90% (U-shaped). Minimum monitoring: trending SpO₂ + CO₂ (capillary/venous); CO₂ ↑ เป็น warning signal ที่มาก่อน hypoxemia — SpO₂ alone ไม่เพียงพอ. Sleep study ช่วย quantify OSA severity + guide intervention decision.
Q: แม่ฝากครรภ์พบ polyhydramnios + micrognathia ใน U/S 30 wk. Delivery room should prepare what?

ตอบ: สงสัย Robin sequence → prenatal involvement ของ neonatologist + ENT ก่อน delivery. Delivery room: NP airway (modified ETT through nares) + video laryngoscope + difficult airway cart ready; positioned: prone/lateral decubitus ทันที; NICU bed warmed; capillary blood gas ใน first 2 hours; feeding therapist available. EXIT procedure ถ้า prenatal imaging shows neck mass with tracheal compression/deviation หรือ solid neck mass.
Q: Neonate อายุ 2 ชั่วโมง มี cyanosis ที่ดีขึ้นเมื่อร้องไห้ + feeding ยาก. วินิจฉัยอะไร? ทำอะไรก่อน?

ตอบ: Bilateral posterior choanal atresia (pathognomonic: cyanosis relieved by crying). First: oral airway ทันที; ถ้าไม่พอ → ETT intubation; CT nasal passages + temporal bones (ยืนยัน + ตรวจหา CHARGE ear: Mondini cochlea + absent/hypoplastic semicircular canals); echo; CBC + Ca + flow cytometry; audiologic + ophthalmologic evaluation. สงสัย CHARGE syndrome (CHD7) จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่.
Q: Infant with ToF + neonatal seizures + hypocalcemia — ต้องทำอะไร?

ตอบ: 22q11.2 deletion syndrome จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่ (1:4,000; most common microdeletion). ส่ง chromosomal microarray หรือ FISH for 22q11.2; treat hypocalcemia urgently (seizure + hypocalcemia → worse intellectual outcome); workup: Ca + PTH, CBC + T/B cell flow cytometry, renal US, thyroid NBS, palatal exam, hearing screen, ophthalmology, genetic counseling.
Q: แยก sagittal synostosis จาก positional (deformational) plagiocephaly ด้วยอะไร?

ตอบ: Sagittal synostosis (scaphocephaly): frontal bossing + biparietal narrowing + occipital elongation ที่ progressive; breech dolichocephaly will normalize ใน first months. Lambdoid synostosis vs positional posterior plagiocephaly: lambdoid synostosis มี skull base tilt + inferior-posterior ear displacement (mastoid prominence ipsilateral) — ไม่มีใน positional plagiocephaly. CT scan ยืนยัน + surgical planning.
Q: BWS neonate แรกคลอด: glucose 1.5 mmol/L หลัง GIR 8 mg/kg/min. Next step?

ตอบ: Refractory hyperinsulinism hypoglycemia ใน BWS; next step: endocrinologist consult + ↑ GIR further; insulin assay + cortisol; diazoxide หรือ subcutaneous octreotide; ถ้าไม่ตอบสนอง → subtotal pancreatectomy พิจารณา. ยังต้องทำ abdominal US ทันที (tumor surveillance baseline) + genetics consult. ECG + echo ถ้าสงสัย cardiac anomaly.
Q: Infant with abnormal head shape — พบ coronal synostosis bilateral. Gene panel อะไรที่ต้องส่ง?

ตอบ: Bilateral coronal synostosis: test FGFR2, FGFR3, TWIST1, TCF12, EFNB1 ตาม clinical exam findings. FGFR3 P250R → Muenke syndrome; FGFR2 → Apert หรือ Crouzon/Pfeiffer (ถ้า proptosis); TWIST1 → Saethre-Chotzen (low hairline, ptosis, 2–3 syndactyly); EFNB1 → craniofrontonasal syndrome (females more severe). Genetics consult ทุกราย; multisuture → exome/WGS ถ้า panel negative.
Q: CNPAS — อธิบาย pathognomonic physical exam findings และ threshold สำหรับ surgical intervention

ตอบ: Pathognomonic features: absent maxillary labial frenulum + solitary median maxillary central incisor (60%); CT: PA < 11 mm term. Surgery: failure of 2-week conservative trial (saline + intranasal steroids + decongestants); or persistent stertor, ↑ WOB, inability to wean respiratory support, failure to thrive, sleep apnea; most cases requiring surgery have PA 4.8–6.6 mm. Sublabial approach — widen until 3.5-mm ETT passes freely. All cases need brain MRI + endocrine evaluation (40% pituitary dysfunction).
Q: Treacher Collins vs Craniofacial Microsomia — แยกอย่างไร? ทำไม TCS มี tracheostomy rate สูงกว่า?

ตอบ: TCS: bilateral + symmetric mandibular + zygomatic hypoplasia + microtia + conductive hearing loss + downslanting palpebral fissures + lower eyelid colobomas; gene TCOF1 (71–93%). CFM: unilateral (usually) + asymmetric jaw + ear involvement (OMENS); extracraniofacial anomalies 47%. TCS tracheostomy rate ↑: bilateral symmetric mandibular hypoplasia + frequent choanal stenosis/atresia → multilevel airway obstruction → MDO ไม่เพียงพอ → tracheostomy often necessary; neonatal death risk.
Day 89 Preview

Avery Chapter 89 — Common Neonatal Orthopedic Conditions
พรุ่งนี้: Common Neonatal Orthopedic Conditions — ต่อเนื่องจาก Part XVIII; หลังเรียน craniofacial disorders วันนี้ เราจะขยับมา musculoskeletal conditions: clubfoot (talipes equinovarus), developmental dysplasia ของ hip (DDH), polydactyly, syndactyly, congenital limb deficiencies, arthrogryposis — เนื้อหาเชื่อมกับ craniofacial (RS มี clubfoot บ่อย; arthrogryposis พบใน NMD + craniofacial syndromes).
